projectDescription: A reference library of minimal Solidity implementations documenting non-standard and dangerous ERC20 token behaviors, intended for smart contract developers and security auditors.
keyFeatures: 21+ minimal Solidity reference implementations of non-standard ERC20 token behaviors
Based on real-world tokens (USDT, BNB, USDC, DAI, UNI, COMP, MKR, etc.)
Covers transfer quirks: missing return values, fee-on-transfer, reentrancy, and more
Covers approval quirks: race protections, zero-value reverts, large-value reverts
Covers admin risks: blocklists, pausable tokens, upgradable proxies
Documents historical DeFi exploits caused by these behaviors
Licensed under AGPL-3.0 and published to npm for easy integration
publicApiSurface: ERC20.sol — Base ERC20 implementation (transfer, transferFrom, approve)
Reentrant.sol — ReentrantToken: calls arbitrary targets on transferFrom
MissingReturns.sol — Token without bool return values on ERC20 methods
ReturnsFalse.sol — Token that returns false even on successful operations
TransferFee.sol — TransferFeeToken: deducts fee on every transfer
BlockList.sol — BlockableToken: admin-controlled address blocklist
Pausable.sol — PausableToken: admin can pause all transfers
Upgradable.sol — Proxy: EIP-1967 upgradable proxy token
Uint96.sol — Token that reverts on values exceeding uint96
Approval.sol — Token with approval race condition protection
ApprovalToZeroAddress.sol — Token that reverts on approve(address(0), amt)
ApprovalWithZeroValue.sol — Token that reverts on approve(address, 0)
RevertZero.sol — Token that reverts on zero-value transfers
RevertToZero.sol — Token that reverts on transfer to address(0)
NoRevert.sol — Token that returns false instead of reverting on failure
LowDecimals.sol — Token with 2 decimal places
HighDecimals.sol — Token with 24 decimal places
Bytes32Metadata.sol — Token with bytes32 name/symbol instead of string
Proxied.sol — Token accessible via multiple proxy addresses
TransferFromSelf.sol — Token that always deducts allowance in transferFrom
DaiPermit.sol — Token with DAI-style permit (non-EIP2612 compliant)
